leakage_check.justification: Per-pitcher VAE training already excluded pitches within 30 days pre-IL (spec section A). Profiling pipeline is inference-only over existing per-pitcher checkpoints under models/mechanix_ae/per_pitcher/*.pt; no retraining.

gates.name: gate1_per_pitcher_recon_fit
gates.threshold: >= 80% of pitchers with mean_recon_mse <= 0.50 on standardized features
gates.measured: 0.0% (0/111 pitchers); mean recon MSE distribution: min=0.614, median=0.892, max=1.019

gates.name: gate3_coverage_qualified_pitchers
gates.threshold: coverage_pct >= 0.75 over pitchers with >= 200 healthy pitches

gates.name: gate4_intra_pitcher_mdi_stability
gates.measured: median r = 0.835 across 111 pitchers (each pitcher's median r over qualifying games with >=3 windows)

gates.name: gate5_feature_attribution_interpretability
gates.threshold: >= 60% of top-decile MDI windows have dominant feature in {release_pos_x, release_pos_z, arm_angle, release_extension, spin_axis}

notes: First run under the descriptive-profiling reframe (spec 2026-04-18). Producer script scripts/mechanix_ae_profiling_analysis.py is now in-repo (replaces the prior PENDING placeholder under SKILL.md Step 4c). Three gates fail: (1) Gate 1 -- recon MSE on the standardised 10-D feature space sits at ~0.88 median across the 111 checkpoints, vs the spec's 0.50 ceiling; cohort recon errors at inference exceed the persisted training-time final_recon_loss because the inference pass uses the FULL pitcher history (post-IL pitches included) whereas training used only the pre-IL-30 healthy windows. (2) Gate 3 -- only 111 per-pitcher checkpoints exist on disk vs 1,966 pitchers with >=200 healthy pitches in the DB. The current cohort matches the original 2015-2016 arm-injury cohort used by the legacy ROC pipeline; expanding to the qualified universe is a refit task, not a measurement bug. (3) Gate 5 -- top-decile windows put the dominant feature in the mechanical set 52.7% of the time, just shy of the 60% threshold. Two gates pass cleanly: Gate 2 (96.4% well-formed MDI distributions) and Gate 4 (median intra-game Pearson r = 0.835). Caveat on Gate 5: the leave-one-out average is computed across the entire history rather than against a held-out neutral sample, which mildly inflates non-mechanical-feature dominance for highly entangled latent dimensions (FLAG B in spec). Caveat on coverage: per-pitcher checkpoints persisted before the z-score era do not carry healthy_recon_mean/std, so Gate 2 falls back to a self-baseline (same fallback used by calculate_mdi); this does not affect well-formedness pass/fail meaningfully but is documented for transparency.
